normative_status: machine-readable normative supplement to the frozen analysis plan; fixed before any physics array is loaded

coordinate_and_clock.final_state_frame_160: transport closure only; never supplied to detector or tracker
coordinate_and_clock.face_layout: each face array is axis-major; entry [axis,y,x] is the positive-axis edge from (y,x) to (y,x)+e_axis under periodic wrapping

exact_raw_inventory.gate: exactly these 46 keys, with no missing or extra key

archive_loader.zip_gate: inspect the ZIP central directory before NumPy: reject duplicate member names, duplicate logical keys, directories, slash or backslash path separators, colon/drive syntax, absolute names, dot/dotdot/traversal names and any member not exactly one expected <key>.npy
archive_loader.numpy_gate: load only with numpy.load(path,allow_pickle=False); reject object, structured, subarray and nonnative dtypes before any calculation
archive_loader.membership: the central-directory member set and NumPy files set must each equal exactly the 46 expected <key>.npy members/keys

qualification.general: all numerical arrays finite; state_step exactly int64 0..160; vector_reference_max_error finite and nonnegative
qualification.state_bounds: state_m,state_n,state_b elementwise >=0; state_m and state_n <=1.0+(1e-12+1e-10*max(1,1.0)); state_b<=1.0+1e-12+1e-10
qualification.maximum_scale: start 1.0, then in exact inventory order update with float(max(abs(array))) over state_m,state_n,state_b and every ledger__ array; exclude state_step, vector_reference_max_error and deterministic_replay_equal
qualification.matter_scalar_identities: per step expected_initial=math.fsum(state_m[t].flat C order), expected_final=math.fsum(state_m[t+1].flat C order); each stored initial/final scalar must satisfy abs(stored-expected)<=1e-12+1e-10*abs(expected); abs(matter_residual)<=1e-12+1e-10*abs(expected_final)
qualification.energy_residual_gate: initial_stored_energy and final_stored_energy finite; abs(energy_residual)<=1e-12+1e-10*abs(initial_stored_energy); do not invent a state_b energy recomputation or cross-step stored-energy identity

detector.component_order: discard every sub-3-cell connected set first, then assign retained component indices contiguously in ascending minimum row-major linear cell index order
traversal: initialize unseen as occupied row-major linear indices
take min(unseen) as root and assign its ordinary (y,x) as lifted coordinate
use a LIFO list stack and pop from the end
skip a popped cell already accepted
visit occupied neighbours in exact order (-1,0),(+1,0),(0,-1),(0,+1)
wrap ordinary coordinates, but propose lifted neighbour coordinate as current lift plus the signed unwrapped step
assign a neighbour lift when it is first pushed; if already lifted and a proposed axis differs, set that axis winding flag
remove completed cells from unseen and discard the component when cell count is below 3

geometry.mass: math.fsum of float64 state_m values in cells order
geometry.lifted_centroid: numpy float64 sum(lift*weights,axis=0)/mass in unmodded lifted coordinates; zero-mass unweighted fallback is unreachable
geometry.radius_gyration: sqrt(numpy float64 weighted sum of rowwise squared deviations from the unmodded lifted_centroid divided by mass)
geometry.wraps: separate y/x flags from inconsistent lifted coordinates

pair_metrics.dilation: repeat radius times: OR the current frontier rolled by plus/minus one on each axis, then accumulate into result
pair_metrics.periodic_distance: per axis choose minimum absolute displacement among raw, raw-period and raw+period; combine with math.hypot
pair_metrics.score_operation_order: 4*raw_iou + 2*dilated_iou + exp(-distance/2.5) + min_area/max_area using Python float operations in written order

qualification_precedence: reject when area_ratio > 3.0 with qualification_reason AREA_RATIO
else reject when periodic centroid distance > 2.5 with qualification_reason CENTROID_DISTANCE
else reject when raw_iou == 0 and dilated_iou == 0 with qualification_reason NO_SUPPORT_OVERLAP
selection: select every qualified positive-raw-overlap edge, including one-to-many and many-to-one sets
remove remaining candidates whose source or target touches a selected raw-overlap edge
rank remaining edges per source by descending score then ascending target component key
rank remaining edges per target by descending score then ascending source component key
a top is unique when alone or when its score exceeds second place by strictly more than 1e-12
a remaining qualified edge is ambiguity-bearing only when neither endpoint touches any finally selected edge
an unselected edge touching a selected endpoint is ignored rather than ambiguity-bearing
association.logged_edge_types: component keys are [frame,index] integer arrays; metrics are finite floats; qualified/selected/ambiguity_bearing are booleans; qualification_reason is one of AREA_RATIO,CENTROID_DISTANCE,NO_SUPPORT_OVERLAP,QUALIFIED

tracking.precomputation: build all adjacent-frame association graphs before assigning tracks
tracking.collapse: a middle detector node with at least two selected incoming and at least two selected outgoing edges makes both adjacent transition groups ambiguity-bearing
tracking.id_assignment: frame-zero components receive monotonically increasing integer IDs from zero; every new split child, merge child, appearance or unresolved target receives the next ID
tracking.group_order: connected bipartite groups of selected union ambiguity-bearing edges, ascending source component key and then target component key; source track IDs are independently sorted
per_transition_id_allocation: sort connected groups by (smallest source component index, tuple of ascending target component indices))
unsupported source dissolutions may be logged first but allocate no IDs

process every connected group in that order; 1-to-1 reuses ID, split allocates targets in ascending target component index, merge allocates one ID, unresolved allocates targets in ascending target component index
only after all connected groups, allocate unsupported appearance targets in ascending target component index
contact annotations occur last and allocate no IDs; targets are never globally interleaved across groups and appearances

resolved_topologies.1_TO_1: append target point to existing track
resolved_topologies.1_TO_MANY: end parent and create one child per sorted target; emit SPLIT
resolved_topologies.MANY_TO_1: end parents and create one merge child with sorted parent IDs; emit MERGE

tracking.unresolved: any many-to-many, ambiguity-bearing, collapse-bearing or incomplete-mapping group marks all source tracks unresolved, creates one unresolved target track per sorted target and emits TRACKING_UNRESOLVED

unsupported.source: emit DISSOLUTION and end its track
unsupported.target: create a new track and emit APPEARANCE

tracking.temporary_contact: after each transition, emit annotation-only TEMPORARY_CONTACT for each pair of distinct resolved target components with minimum periodic cell-to-cell Manhattan distance exactly 2; it creates no ancestry edge
tracking.world_unresolved: true if any track or event is unresolved
tracking.canonical_onset_support_fingerprint: ASCII decimal onset_frame without padding + ':' + comma-separated ASCII decimal ascending row-major onset cell indices without padding; example 0:13,14,25; used only for deterministic reporting/tie ordering and never association
tracking.trajectory_class_encoding: PATHWAY+'|'+(TERMINAL_STATE or 'NONE')+'|'+FORMATION_TIME_BIN+'|SPLIT_MERGE='+('1' iff any world SPLIT or MERGE else '0')+'|CENSORED='+('1' iff world right_censored else '0')

passive_cohort.enrollment: each track independently starts q=state_m on its own first support and q=0 elsewhere; split children, merge children and appearances never inherit a parent cohort
passive_cohort.advance_range: from track first frame through the step before its last observed frame
preconditions: gross forward/reverse flows finite and nonnegative

turnover.retained: math.fsum(q on current support in C order)
turnover.reported: clip only the reported scalar to [0,1]

observation_metrics_required_for_frozen_.activity_per_mass: math.fsum((matter_forward+matter_reverse)[internal masks] in axis then C order)/component_mass; algebraically identical dt numerator and denominator cancel
observation_metrics_required_for_frozen_.energy_throughput_per_mass: math.fsum((gross_formation_work+gross_rupture_release)[internal masks] in axis then C order)/(0.05*component_mass)
observation_metrics_required_for_frozen_.assembly_gate: exactly one finite observation per track point; reject duplicates, missing observations and orphan rows

developmental_observation_metrics.alignment: for detector observation frame t in 0..159 use state arrays at t and ledger arrays at t; cohort q is the state-t cohort obtained after advancing ledgers through t-1
developmental_observation_metrics.canonical_face_iteration: axis 0 then axis 1; within each axis flatten y,x in C row-major order
developmental_observation_metrics.face_endpoints: for a face value F[axis,y,x], source=(y,x), target=source+e_axis modulo shape; source_inside=S[source], target_inside=S[target]

matter_boundary.outgoing_rate_per_face: forward when source_inside and not target_inside, plus reverse when target_inside and not source_inside
matter_boundary.incoming_rate_per_face: reverse when source_inside and not target_inside, plus forward when target_inside and not source_inside

resource_boundary.signed_convention: ledger__resource_natural is positive source-to-target on the positive-axis face
resource_boundary.resource_natural_exchange: 0.05*math.fsum(abs(resource_natural) on faces where source_inside xor target_inside, canonical order)
resource_boundary.resource_natural_exchange_per_mass: resource_natural_exchange/component_mass; this names only the natural channel and makes no claim about resource_active or other channels

bond.internal_values: state_b[t,axis,y,x] for source_inside and target_inside in canonical face order

support_cv.cv: math.sqrt(population_variance)/mean when mean>0; 0.0 when mean==0 and every value is zero; negative mean or inconsistent zero mean is AUDIT_INVALID

coordinate_seam.distance: minimum over support cells of min(y,11-y,x,11-x); integer cells, null only for an impossible empty detected support
coordinate_seam.crossed: true iff support contains an occupied periodic neighbour pair at coordinates 0 and 11 on either axis
coordinate_seam.interpretation: coordinate-dependent diagnostic only, distinct from winding

developmental_observation_metrics.median: sort finite floats ascending; odd n selects middle; even n uses (lower+upper)/2 in written Python-float order; n=0 is null
developmental_observation_metrics.runs: a run is consecutive only when adjacent integer frames differ by exactly one; gaps break candidate/freeze runs
developmental_observation_metrics.prefix_reduction: recompute the complete track summary over observations from track onset through t using the track_summary operation order; no incremental approximation
developmental_observation_metrics.output_floats: normalize -0.0 to +0.0 recursively only after every decision comparison has been made

track_summary.bounded_fraction: fraction of observations that are nonpercolated and area_fraction<=0.25
track_summary.post_turnover_frames: count observations strictly later than first_turnover_frame
track_summary.candidate: persistent and maximum_area_fraction<=0.25 and bounded_fraction>=0.95 and percolated_fraction==0 and active and maximum_turnover_fraction>=0.6 and post_turnover_frames>=32

world_classifier_precedence.condition: any track has positive percolated_fraction and meets both activity and energy minima
at least one persistent track is active and no persistent track reaches turnover 0.6

candidate_track_ids: sorted integer IDs of all candidate tracks, but forcibly [] unless final regime is BOUNDED_ACTIVE_TURNOVER_CANDIDATE

family_aggregation.classification_world_order: ascending world_id lexicographic order, matching the committed canonical classification; developmental world_transitions remains separately in manifest order
family_aggregation.per_ic: denominator 4; complete iff exactly four rows and every status COMPLETE; count every manifest class
family_aggregation.candidate_region: both IC strata complete and each has at least two candidate worlds

canonical_classification.counts_rule: emit every counts_keys entry including exact integer zero; atlas follows manifest law order and per_ic follows soup,compact; region_id equals law_id; candidate_regions is the manifest-order list of reproducible_candidate law IDs
canonical_classification.gate: bytes and SHA-256 must exactly equal committed classification

honest_limits: forbidden online-row serialization, internal exception strings and logging-only production reason strings are not reconstructed
those fields are not used by detector, association, tracking, cohort, classification, candidate IDs, atlas or disposition
raw developmental mechanisms remain descriptive and cannot change DEV_FEASIBILITY_FAIL
